name: Early Life & Indian Wandering
description: Born in Kolkata, spiritual awakening under Sri Ramakrishna, and six years of wandering as a parivrajaka (wandering monk) across the length and breadth of India — discovering the land, its people, and crystallising his vision for India's spiritual renaissance.

name: First Western Tour & Parliament of Religions
description: Vivekananda's historic journey to the West — through Ceylon, Malaysia, Singapore, China, Japan and Canada — culminating in his electrifying speech at the Parliament of World's Religions in Chicago on 11 September 1893. He spent nearly four years spreading Vedanta across the USA and England.

name: Return to India & Mission Building
description: Triumphant return to India greeted by massive crowds. Founded the Ramakrishna Mission (1897) and Belur Math. Travelled across India to inaugurate centres, deliver lectures, and inspire a generation. Also visited Himalayas for spiritual retreat.

name: Second Western Tour
description: Second visit to the West — USA, England, France (Congress of Religions, 1900) and Egypt — despite deteriorating health. Established Vedanta Society of San Francisco and deepened contacts in Paris before returning via Cairo.

name: Final Years in India
description: Returned to Belur Math for his final years. Made last pilgrimage tours to Varanasi, Bodh Gaya and other sacred sites. Attained Mahasamadhi on 4 July 1902 at Belur Math at age 39.
